question: what did I say about the saddle?
notes: single-fact lookup; the recall() docstring's canonical retrieval example

question: where do I live now?
notes: one current belief; first-person form of the seed 'Where does the user live currently?'

question: what do I currently believe about sushi?
notes: current-belief lookup; no evolution or aggregation intent

question: what web framework do I prefer these days?
notes: one current preference

question: how old is my daughter now?
notes: 'how old' is not an aggregation marker; one current fact

question: what car do I drive?
notes: one current fact

question: where do I work out currently?
notes: one current fact

question: what does my partner do for work now?
notes: one current fact about a named person

question: what did I decide about the house?
notes: positive counterpart of the absence class: the decision EXISTS and is one belief

question: what's my current stance on remote work?
notes: current stance = one belief; no evolution marker, so not a narrative walk

question: which gym did I join?
notes: one past fact, still current

question: what did I tell you about the deposit?
notes: content lookup; contrast with rt-bnd-14, which asks EXISTENCE of any mention

question: am I still training for the marathon?
notes: current-status check on a known positive belief ('Is the user currently training?') [gold corrected 2026-07-08: 'am/do I still X' is AbsenceEval's still-family shape (pradhvamsabhava); retrieval kept as acceptable secondary — inter-instrument consistency]

question: what's my company called now?
notes: one current fact, supersession-aware retrieval's bread and butter

question: how much did I spend on the bike in total?
notes: sum over the preserved tuple index; top-K retrieval is the wrong primitive

question: how many books did I finish this year?
notes: count of discrete events; exhaustive arithmetic

question: what's the average I paid per physio session?
notes: average op (the 59.6-avg LongMemEval shape)

question: what's the most I've ever paid for a flight?
notes: max op over amounts; 'ever' here scopes the max, it is not an absence probe

question: how much more did I spend on rent this year than last year?
notes: difference op, two scoped sums

question: what's my combined spend on climbing gear?
notes: sum op ('combined')

question: how many concerts did I go to in 2025?
notes: count op with a year scope

question: what did the kitchen renovation cost altogether?
notes: sum op ('altogether')

question: what's the least I paid for a hotel night on the japan trip?
notes: min op scoped to one trip

question: how much have I put into the emergency fund overall?
notes: sum op ('overall')

question: on average, how much do I spend on groceries a week?
notes: average op with a period unit; still ONE number, not a per-period series

question: how many sessions with the trainer have I logged?
notes: count op

question: what's the total of my charity donations this year?
notes: sum op ('total')

question: how much did the two camera lenses cost combined?
notes: sum over an enumerated pair (the $270 two-item LongMemEval shape)

question: how has my thinking about bouldering evolved?
notes: evolution op; the canonical dev-set phrasing, fresh domain

question: how has my view of the promotion changed over time?
notes: perspective_shift phrasing, fresh domain

question: trace how my relationship with alcohol has gone back and forth
notes: reversed_belief_chain phrasing, fresh domain

question: when did I first start thinking about moving abroad?
notes: origin op ('when did I first start')

question: how did I get into fermentation?
notes: origin op ('how did I get into')

question: what got me into trail running?
notes: origin op ('what got me into')

question: trace my views on homeschooling
notes: throughline op ('trace my views')

question: what's the arc of my thinking on electric cars?
notes: throughline op ('the arc')

question: how has my stance on open source licensing shifted?
notes: evolution op ('shifted'); non-quantified theme so not composition

question: where did my obsession with mechanical keyboards come from?
notes: origin op ('where did my X come from')

question: what's the thread running through my career choices?
notes: throughline op ('the thread running through')

question: how has my pottery routine changed and why?
notes: multi_factor_change phrasing, fresh domain; 'and why' wants the causal beat, not a number

question: the first time I mentioned burnout, what was going on?
notes: origin op ('the first time I'); wants origin beat plus its context

question: how have my thoughts on therapy developed?
notes: evolution op ('developed')

question: how has my spending on the bike evolved?
notes: roadmap item 3's canonical example: aggregation subject + evolution marker = time-series of sums

question: how has my monthly grocery spend changed over the year?
notes: explicit per-month bucketing of a spend series

question: what has my gym spending looked like month by month?
notes: 'month by month' = per-period sums, narrative shape over synthesis content

question: how did my book buying change across the seasons?
notes: per-period counts of purchases; quantified theme + change marker

question: show me my coffee spending over time
notes: 'over time' on a spend quantity = the series, not one sum

question: how has the amount I save each month trended this year?
notes: monthly savings series with a trend statement

question: is my eating-out spend going up or down month over month?
notes: direction-of-trend over per-month sums

question: how did my donation totals evolve quarter by quarter?
notes: 'totals' + 'evolve quarter by quarter': per-quarter sums, not one grand total

question: how has my weekly running mileage progressed since january?
notes: per-week quantity series; 'progressed' is the evolution marker

question: what's the trend in my subscription costs over the past year?
notes: trend over a cost series

question: how has what I spend on the kids' activities changed per term?
notes: per-term spend buckets with a change question

question: break down my medical expenses by month this year
notes: imperative per-month breakdown; the answer is the bucketed series itself

question: what have I not decided about the move?
notes: roadmap item 4's canonical example; answer grounded in absence after qualified search

question: have I ever lived abroad?
notes: roadmap item 4 phrasing; atyantabhava 'ever' scoping

question: is there anything I haven't tried for the back pain?
notes: roadmap item 4 phrasing; absence within an enumerable set of attempts

question: what haven't I figured out about the wedding yet?
notes: pragabhava 'yet' scoping: not-yet-decided items

question: have I ever mentioned my grandfather?
notes: existence-of-mention; a NO must be a qualified-search absence claim

question: did I ever follow up with the accountant?
notes: existence check on an action; the risky answer is the confident NO

question: is there any part of the business plan I never finished?
notes: absence over subparts of a known artifact

question: what topics have I never brought up with my manager?
notes: 'never' over a topic set; pure anupalabdhi

question: have I ever been to japan?
notes: 'ever' existence check on a life event

question: is there anything I still haven't booked for the trip?
notes: pradhvamsabhava/'still' scoping over a checklist

question: have I ever changed a bike tire myself?
notes: 'ever' existence check; bike domain deliberately shared with synthesis/boundary rows

question: what did I never get around to reading from last year's list?
notes: list-minus-done absence; the answer names what is missing

question: what does this remind me of?
notes: roadmap item 5's canonical upamana phrasing

question: have I been in a situation like this before?
notes: roadmap item 5 phrasing; situation-similarity, not fact lookup

question: what past project was most similar to this one?
notes: 'most similar' is a similarity superlative, not a numeric max — a designed trap for the ganita 'most' marker

question: does this job offer resemble any decision I've faced before?
notes: explicit resemblance question across episodes

question: what's the closest thing in my past to this negotiation?
notes: nearest-analogue request

question: have I dealt with a client like this before?
notes: 'like this before' — analogue over past episodes, not existence of one fact

question: which previous trip felt most like this one?
notes: 'most like' similarity superlative; second designed trap for the ganita 'most' marker

question: what earlier argument does this conflict with my landlord mirror?
notes: 'mirror' = structural analogy between two episodes

question: is this like the time I switched teams?
notes: candidate analogue named by the user; the job is similarity assessment

question: have I spent anything on the bike?
notes: existence-of-spending: a NO is grounded in absence after a qualified scan of the tuple index; a YES degenerates to a sum — genuinely two-pramana

question: when did I first buy bike gear?
notes: origin question ('when did I first') over purchase events; the first bucket of the spend series answers it too

question: how many times have I changed my mind about remote work?
notes: surface form is a count, but the count exists only after the supersession chain is assembled — the revisions ARE the narrative walk's output

question: how many books did I mention last month?
notes: count-phrased but answerable only from text mentions; recall() docstring names exactly this fall-through (ganita has no mention tuples)

question: how has my coffee budget changed month to month?
notes: quantified theme + explicit per-period evolution = composition; drop the per-month reading and it is a narrative evolution question

question: have I ever tried meditation?
notes: 'ever' existence check (atyantabhava scoping); if a positive belief exists, plain retrieval serves the YES

question: is this apartment hunt going like the last one?
notes: cross-episode similarity between two hunts; also readable as one theme's continuing arc

question: what happened the last time I felt this burned out?
notes: situation-similarity lookup ('this burned out') whose deliverable is a past-episode retrieval

question: how much more am I spending on rent than I used to?
notes: difference op; 'than I used to' implies the per-period series behind the two operands

question: when did I stop drinking coffee?
notes: a single transition fact the supersession record serves without a walk; cessation is not an origin form

question: is there a pattern in how I pick apartments?
notes: throughline across one's own history; nearly an analogy over repeated episodes of the same kind

question: what's the total I've spent since I got serious about cycling?
notes: boundary by flavor, unambiguous by meaning: the narrative clause only scopes the window; the answer is one number

question: how does my spending this spring compare with last autumn?
notes: difference of two scoped sums; a two-bucket series is the same computation read as composition

question: did I ever say anything about the deposit?
notes: existence-of-mention: NO requires qualified-search absence; YES is retrieval (contrast rt-retr-12, which asks for the content)

question: what's still on the list I haven't bought for the nursery?
notes: list-minus-purchases: the absence is the answer, but the list itself is retrieved — two-pramana by construction
